scope: Tier-3 platform + API excellence, cycle 3 of 3 (per project_propmanager_path_forward.md, closing Tier 3). Phase 25 [API-SURFACE] + Phase 26 [MOBILE + PWA] are closed (18/18 each, zero deferrals). Phase 27 [REPORTING + BI] takes PropManager's analytics from 'flat dashboard with 4 fixed report types' to 'world-class BI for institutional landlords'. Today's surface (ReportService + ReportsController + FinanceReportController + Api\ReportController): 4 fixed report types (financial / occupancy / arrears / water), period selector {week/month/quarter/year}, CSV+PDF export, NO cohort analysis, NO NOI/cap-rate, NO forecasting, NO custom report builder, NO scheduled delivery, NO real xlsx (CSV with .xlsx extension is the closest), NO regression tests on the report SQL. Institutional landlords with 200+ units evaluate property management software on these missing capabilities — without them PropManager caps at the small-landlord segment. 18 findings across 6 categories: BI-COHORT (3 — tenant cohort retention curve, monthly acquisition cohort, lifetime-value calculation), BI-NOI (3 — NOI per property/portfolio, cap rate calculator, expense-to-property allocation methodology), BI-FORECAST (3 — rent-roll forecasting, seasonality adjustment, vacancy projection), BI-BUILDER (3 — saved-report library, drag-drop field picker, SQL generator with safety filter), BI-DELIVERY (3 — xlsx export via PhpSpreadsheet, scheduled email delivery, configurable daily/weekly/monthly cadence), BI-CI (3 — golden-query regression test, report query performance budget, new-report-type ratchet). Severity 5H/8M/5L. Builds on Phase-22 PERF-SLO (queries-budget pattern), Phase-25 ERROR-1 (problem+json for any failed report endpoint), Phase-26 OFFLINE (cached fallback when the landlord opens a saved report offline), Phase-24 i18n (xlsx column headers + scheduled email subjects localised). Tools: phpoffice/phpspreadsheet for real xlsx (writes formatting + formulas; CSV is not xlsx), Laravel Scheduler for cadence, existing Mail infra for delivery.

today_reports.service: app/Services/ReportService.php (~700 lines, monolithic — all 4 report types in one class)

today_exports.xlsx: FAUX — ReportsController::exportExcel uses fputcsv and renames to .xlsx; opens in Excel as CSV (no formatting, no formulas, no real cell types)

today_missing.saved_reports: NONE — reports are query-on-demand, no persistence layer
today_missing.custom_builder: NONE — landlord cannot choose fields, can only pick from 4 fixed types
today_missing.scheduled_delivery: NONE — there is no SendScheduledReport mailable, no daily/weekly cron schedule for reports
today_missing.regression_tests: NONE — no Phase26-style watchdog asserts the SQL output shape for the 4 existing types

today_deps.chart_lib: Chart.js used in Vue (PWA-NETWORK-2 already gates this for slow connections)

findings.title: Tenant cohort retention curve — survival rate by acquisition month
findings.evidence: Institutional landlords measure churn via cohort retention: 'of the tenants acquired in Jan 2025, what % are still active at month 3 / 6 / 12?'. Without this they can't tell if a building improvement campaign changed churn or if a new contract template improved retention. The query is a self-join on leases grouped by start_date YYYY-MM with COUNT alive at each (current_month - cohort_month) offset.
findings.fix: (a) CohortService::retentionMatrix(landlordId, lookbackMonths) returns matrix[cohortMonth][offsetMonth] = survivalPct; (b) Cohort.vue renders a triangular heatmap (vue-chartjs already in deps); (c) drilldown on each cell lists the leases that ended in that offset; (d) Phase27CohortTest::test_retention_matrix_shape + test_diagonal_is_one (self-month always 100%).

findings.title: Monthly acquisition cohort table — net new + churned + reactivated
findings.evidence: Counter-balance to BI-COHORT-1: the absolute counts per month — how many new tenants started, how many churned, net delta. Surfaces growth trajectory the retention curve obscures (a 90% retention rate on a shrinking portfolio is still shrinking).
findings.fix: (a) acquisitionTable(landlordId, fromMonth, toMonth) returns [{month, new_leases, churned_leases, reactivated, net_delta}]; (b) UI sortable table + net-delta column conditional formatting; (c) Phase27CohortTest::test_acquisition_table_balances (sum(new) - sum(churned) + sum(reactivated) == active_at_end - active_at_start).

findings.title: Tenant lifetime value (LTV) calculation per cohort
findings.evidence: LTV = sum(payments) per tenant over the lease lifetime, aggregated per acquisition cohort. Operators use this to evaluate the unit economics of marketing campaigns + tenant-acquisition cost. The methodology doc matters here — there's no single LTV formula; PropManager picks 'sum of all payments excluding refunds, attributed to the cohort month the lease started'.
findings.fix: (a) lifetimeValue(landlordId, cohortMonth) returns {tenants_count, total_payments_kes, mean_ltv_kes, median_ltv_kes}; (b) docs/runbooks/bi.md documents the calculation + edge cases (subletting, lease renewal, refunds); (c) Phase27CohortTest::test_ltv_excludes_refunds.

findings.title: Net Operating Income (NOI) per property + portfolio
findings.evidence: NOI = (rental income - operating expenses) per property, the canonical real-estate operating metric. Today PropManager has revenue + expense data but no per-property NOI. Institutional landlords sort properties by NOI to identify under-performers; lenders demand NOI in covenant reports.
findings.fix: (a) NoiService::byProperty(landlordId, period) joins payments, expenses (per Phase-22 PERF-CACHE-1 query patterns), groups by property_id; (b) Portfolio-level NOI = sum across properties; (c) UI Noi.vue ranks properties + shows NOI margin + occupancy; (d) Phase27NoiTest::test_noi_matches_revenue_minus_expenses + test_unallocated_expenses_show_in_portfolio_only.

findings.title: Cap rate calculator — NOI / property value, with comparable benchmark
findings.evidence: Cap rate = NOI / property value, expressed as a percentage. 6-8% is typical for Kenyan residential, 8-12% commercial. Investors use it to compare property performance against alternative investments (T-bill yield, REIT dividends). Requires a property.estimated_value field which doesn't exist yet — adding it is in-scope for this finding.
findings.fix: (a) properties.estimated_value decimal(12,2) nullable + ALTER migration; (b) Properties/Edit.vue UI field (with i18n keys); (c) NoiService::capRate(landlordId) = annualised NOI / estimated_value per property; (d) UI displays cap rate with comparable benchmark band (6-8% green, <6% amber, >8% green); (e) Phase27NoiTest::test_cap_rate_annualises_partial_period_correctly.

findings.title: Expense-to-property allocation methodology + UI
findings.evidence: Expenses today are recorded against a building or 'general'. A general expense (e.g. landlord's accountant fee) must be allocated across properties for accurate per-property NOI. Three valid methods: per-unit (split by unit count), per-revenue (split by revenue share), per-floor-area (when applicable). Currently no allocation — general expenses inflate the portfolio NOI but understate per-property NOI.
findings.fix: (a) expenses.allocation_method enum {direct, per_unit, per_revenue, per_floor_area}; (b) UI picker on Expenses/Edit.vue; (c) NoiService allocates per the chosen method when grouping; (d) docs/runbooks/bi.md documents each method's formula + when to use which; (e) Phase27NoiTest::test_per_unit_allocation_sums_to_expense_total.

findings.title: Rent-roll forecasting — 1/3/6/12 month expected revenue
findings.evidence: Cash-flow forecasting is the #1 ask from landlords with >50 units (per the 2026-Q1 sales feedback log). Today the landlord adds it up mentally from lease rents. The forecast must reflect: (a) currently active leases, (b) leases ending in the forecast window (assume not renewed unless a renewal is signed), (c) vacant units (assume no income unless filled), (d) late-payer probability (collection rate from history).
findings.fix: (a) ForecastService::rentRoll(landlordId, months) returns [{month, expected_revenue, low_estimate, high_estimate}]; (b) low_estimate = active_leases * historical_collection_rate; high_estimate = active_leases + (vacant_units * avg_rent * vacancy_fill_rate); (c) UI Forecast.vue line chart + confidence band; (d) Phase27ForecastTest::test_active_lease_full_in_first_month + test_lease_ending_falls_off_at_end_date.

findings.title: Seasonality adjustment — quarterly/holiday revenue patterns
findings.evidence: Kenya has December holiday + January 'tenant moves out' patterns. A naive forecast underestimates December revenue (tenants pay early for January before travelling) and overestimates January revenue (some units sit vacant). Apply a seasonality factor derived from the landlord's own 3-year history if available, else fall back to a Kenya-wide default.
findings.fix: (a) ForecastService::seasonalityFactor(landlordId, month) = mean(revenue / annualMean) for that month over last 3 years; (b) When < 12 months history, return 1.0; (c) Phase27ForecastTest::test_seasonality_factor_defaults_to_one_for_new_landlord.

findings.title: Vacancy projection — empty units + expected fill date
findings.evidence: Vacancy is the biggest revenue risk. Today the landlord sees the count of vacant units; they don't see expected fill date or lost-revenue projection. Use mean-time-to-fill from the past year (lease_start_date - unit_vacancy_start_date) to project fill date for each current vacancy.
findings.fix: (a) vacancyProjection(landlordId) returns [{unit_id, vacant_since, expected_fill_date, lost_revenue_kes}]; (b) UI Vacancy tab ranks units by lost-revenue; (c) Phase27ForecastTest::test_fill_date_uses_landlords_own_history.

findings.title: Saved-report library — store landlord-configured reports
findings.evidence: Today every report query runs ad-hoc. A landlord configuring 'arrears > 30 days, grouped by building, sorted by amount' must reconfigure on every visit. Saved reports persist {name, type, filters, group_by, sort_by, fields[]} so the landlord opens them with one click.
findings.fix: (a) saved_reports table (landlord_id FK, name, type, config JSON, created_at, updated_at); (b) SavedReport model with TenantScope; (c) SavedReportPolicy + registration in AuthServiceProvider; (d) Phase27BuilderTest::test_saved_reports_are_scoped_to_landlord.

findings.title: Drag-drop field picker UI for custom report builder
findings.evidence: The landlord must be able to PICK which fields appear in a report without writing SQL. The picker offers a curated allowlist (lease.rent_amount, payment.amount, tenant.name, unit.number, etc.) and the user drags to the column or filter zones. SQL is GENERATED — never user-typed.
findings.fix: (a) Builder.vue with three zones (columns, filters, group_by); (b) FieldPicker.vue lists the allowlist with categories (Leases / Payments / Tenants / Units); (c) Drag-drop via SortableJS (already in deps via vueuse); (d) Phase27BuilderTest::test_field_allowlist_is_locked (a unit test on the PHP side that asserts the curated list matches docs/runbooks/bi.md).

findings.title: Safe SQL generator — no user-typed SQL ever reaches the DB
findings.evidence: Custom report builder is a classic SQL-injection surface. The defence-in-depth posture is: the user picks from an allowlist of fields/operators/values, the service generates parameterised Eloquent queries (never string-concat SQL), and a regression test asserts injection payloads are rejected at the validation layer. Phase-1 SQLi cleanup (commit 03e17b7) is the standard we maintain.
findings.fix: (a) ReportBuilderService::run(config) validates config against the allowlist + builds an Eloquent query (->where with bound parameters only); (b) ANY field/operator/value outside the allowlist throws ValidationException at the entrance; (c) Phase27BuilderInjectionTest fires 20+ classic injection payloads at the field/operator/value slots + asserts they all reject; (d) docs/runbooks/bi.md documents the allowlist as a contract — adding a field requires adding the allowlist entry.

findings.title: Real xlsx export via PhpSpreadsheet — formatting + formulas + cell types
findings.evidence: Today's 'xlsx' export is CSV with a renamed extension. Excel opens it but treats every cell as text; KES amounts can't be summed without re-typing as Number; dates display as 'Apr 5 2026' string. Institutional landlords import these into existing dashboards and demand real xlsx (proper cell types + currency formatting + header styling).
findings.fix: (a) phpoffice/phpspreadsheet ^3.0 added; (b) XlsxExportService::write($report, $path) writes typed cells (Numeric + DateTimeImmutable + Currency format mask 'KES #,##0.00'); (c) Bold header row + auto-width columns; (d) Phase27ExportTest::test_xlsx_amount_column_is_numeric_type + test_currency_format_mask_is_kes.

findings.title: Scheduled report email delivery — Mailable + Schedule cadence
findings.evidence: Landlord doesn't want to log in every Monday to see the weekly arrears report — they want it in their inbox at 06:00. Today no such infrastructure exists. The scheduled_reports table records {saved_report_id, cadence, delivery_email, last_sent_at, next_due_at}; the daily artisan command finds next_due_at <= now() and queues a job per report.
findings.fix: (a) scheduled_reports table; (b) ScheduledReport model with belongsTo(SavedReport); (c) ScheduledReportDelivery mailable (Phase-24 HasLocalePreference compliant — subject + body in recipient locale); (d) SendScheduledReports command iterates due reports + queues mailable with rendered xlsx attachment; (e) Schedule::command('reports:send-scheduled')->dailyAt('06:00'); (f) Phase27DeliveryTest::test_due_reports_send_xlsx_attachment + test_next_due_at_advances_correctly.

findings.title: Configurable cadence + recipient list per saved report
files: lang/{en,sw}.json (offline.report.* keys would parity-fail; new key prefix `reports.schedule.*`)
findings.evidence: BI-DELIVERY-2 ships the cron + mailable; this finding is the landlord-facing UI to schedule {weekly/monthly/quarterly}, pick the saved report, set the recipient list. Without this UI an operator must edit DB rows directly.
findings.fix: (a) Scheduled.vue lists existing schedules with edit/cancel + 'New schedule' button; (b) Cadence picker {weekly, monthly, quarterly}; (c) Recipient list with email validation (Phase-13 PERSONAL-DATA-1 compliance — only landlord's own email or known caretaker emails); (d) i18n keys reports.schedule.{title, cadence_weekly, cadence_monthly, ...}; (e) Phase27DeliveryTest::test_recipient_list_rejects_third_party_emails.

findings.title: Golden-query regression watchdog — assert SQL output shape for each report type
findings.evidence: Today's 4 report types (financial / occupancy / arrears / water) have no regression tests. A refactor that drops a column or changes a join condition ships invisibly. The watchdog seeds a known dataset, runs each report, and asserts the JSON output matches the committed golden file. Diff = fail.
findings.fix: (a) Phase27GoldenQueriesTest::test_each_report_type_matches_golden_output; (b) Seeded dataset in the test creates 3 properties + 10 units + 8 leases + 20 payments with deterministic IDs; (c) Golden output committed at tests/Fixtures/reports/expected/{type}.json; (d) Re-baseline command: php artisan reports:write-golden (operator runs after an intentional change, commits the new goldens with a one-line why).

findings.title: Report query performance budget — slow-query gate on report endpoints
findings.evidence: Phase-22 PERF-SLO ships an http_request_ms budget for general endpoints. Report endpoints have different SLOs because a 5-year cohort analysis is genuinely slower than a dashboard read. Per-report budgets: cohort 1.5s, NOI 1.0s, forecast 1.0s, builder-custom 2.5s. Test asserts each endpoint's median over 10 runs is under budget.
findings.fix: (a) config/perf.php['report_query_budget_ms'] map; (b) Phase27PerfTest::test_each_report_endpoint_is_within_budget seeds the dataset + measures median; (c) Failure surfaces which report regressed + by how much; (d) Re-baseline by editing config/perf.php with a one-line rationale in the commit.

findings.title: New-report-type ratchet — adding a report requires updating goldens
findings.evidence: Six months from now a new contributor adds a 5th report type but skips the golden file. Phase27GoldenQueriesTest doesn't fail because the new type isn't iterated. This ratchet asserts every report_type returned by ReportService::supportedTypes() has a golden file — forces the discipline.
findings.fix: (a) Phase27CoverageTest::test_every_supported_report_type_has_a_golden_fixture walks ReportService::supportedTypes() + asserts tests/Fixtures/reports/expected/{type}.json exists; (b) Phase27CoverageTest::test_every_supported_report_type_has_a_perf_budget walks supportedTypes() + asserts config/perf.php['report_query_budget_ms'] has the key; (c) the watchdog is the contract — extending it without these is a CI failure.

commits: 4d50f4c — Phase 1b: BI-NOI-1/2/3 (NoiService byProperty + capRate + expense allocation methods)
a7fb1ca — Phase 1d: BI-BUILDER-1/2/3 (saved_reports + Builder.vue + SAFE SQL generator + 20-payload SQLi regression)
8b036a0 — Phase 2: BI-DELIVERY-1/2/3 + BI-CI-1/2/3 (PhpSpreadsheet xlsx + scheduled mailable + dailyAt cron + goldens + perf budget + ratchet)

test_classes_listing: Phase27CohortTest (10 tests) — retentionMatrix shape + diagonal = 100% + acquisition table balances + LTV excludes refunds
Phase27NoiTest (11 tests) — NOI = revenue − expenses + per-property ranking + cap-rate Kenyan band + allocation method sums
Phase27ForecastTest (11 tests) — active lease full in month 1 + lease end falls off + seasonality default = 1.0 + vacancy fill date from history
Phase27BuilderInjectionTest (15 tests / 178 assertions) — 20+ classic SQLi payloads rejected at field/operator/value slots; UNION SELECT, DROP TABLE, comment-strip, time-based blind, etc.
Phase27BuilderTest (10 tests) — SavedReport TenantScope + policy + Builder.vue + field allowlist locked to runbook
Phase27XlsxTest (2 tests) — typed cells with currency/date masks + header bold + brand fill
Phase27ScheduledDeliveryTest (10 tests) — due rows queue mail + next_due_at advances + recipient allowlist (Phase-13 PERSONAL-DATA-1) + dry-run + console schedule registered
Phase27GoldenQueriesTest (4 tests) — each of {financial, occupancy, arrears, water} matches committed golden JSON (with __VARYING__ normalisation for date_range/period/generated_at)
Phase27PerfTest (4 tests) — median of 5 runs per report type within config/perf.php['report_query_budget_ms']
Phase27CoverageTest (2 tests) — every ReportService::supportedTypes() entry has a golden fixture + perf budget

services: app/Services/Reports/CohortService.php (retentionMatrix triangular + acquisitionTable identity + lifetimeValue excluding refunds)
app/Services/Reports/NoiService.php (byProperty + portfolio + capRate annualised + four allocation methods: direct, per_unit, per_revenue, per_floor_area)
app/Services/Reports/ForecastService.php (rentRoll low/expected/high + seasonalityFactor with 3-yr history + vacancyProjection with mean time-to-fill clamped [7,180])
app/Services/Reports/ReportBuilderService.php (SAFE SQL generator — allowlist tables/fields/operators + mandatory landlord_id WHERE + parameterised Eloquent only, never DB::raw with user input)

app/Services/Reports/XlsxExportService.php (PhpSpreadsheet — typed cells, KES currency mask '[$KES] #,##0.00', date 'dd-mmm-yyyy', bold #1F2937 header, auto-fit columns, 31-char title truncation, empty-columns short-circuit)
models_migrations: app/Models/SavedReport.php (TenantScope, casts config as array, registered SavedReportPolicy)
app/Models/ScheduledReport.php (CADENCES = [weekly, monthly, quarterly] + markSent() advances next_due_at)
saved_reports table (landlord_id + name + type + config JSON)
scheduled_reports table (landlord_id + saved_report_id + cadence + recipient_email + next_due_at + last_sent_at)
console_cron: app/Console/Commands/WriteReportGoldens.php (operator runs after intentional change, normalises date_range/period/generated_at → __VARYING__)
fixtures_config: database/seeders/GoldenReportFixtureSeeder.php (anchored to CarbonImmutable::parse('2026-01-01'), 2 properties × 2 units × 3 payments, deterministic)
artifacts_shipped.i18n: lang/{en,sw}/emails.php — scheduled_report subject keys with Swahili parity (Phase-24 gate verified green); reports.schedule.* + reports.builder.* prefixes in Builder.vue + Scheduled.vue
artifacts_shipped.runbook: docs/runbooks/bi.md — cohort methodology (LTV excludes refunds) + NOI allocation formulas + forecast clamps + builder allowlist contract + delivery cadence schema + CI gates (goldens + perf + ratchet)
artifacts_shipped.security_posture: Phase-1 SQLi cleanup (commit 03e17b7) standard maintained: ReportBuilderService validates every config entry against ALLOWED_TABLES + ALLOWED_FIELDS + operator allowlists at the boundary, then only emits parameterised ->where() calls. Phase27BuilderInjectionTest fires 20+ classic SQLi payloads (UNION SELECT, DROP TABLE, OR 1=1, comment-strip, time-based blind, stacked queries, encoded variants) at every input slot and asserts all reject. Phase-13 PERSONAL-DATA-1 compliance: ScheduledController allowlist rejects third-party recipient emails (landlord own + caretaker only).

audit_closeout.next_phase: Phase 28 [TENANT-PORTAL] — Tier 4 cycle 1: dedicated tenant-facing surface (login, lease + balance, payment history, invoice download, maintenance request, document repository, push notifications). Per project_propmanager_path_forward.md Tier 4 (Product depth: tenant + caretaker + ops workflows).
audit_closeout.tier_marker: TIER 3 COMPLETE — Phase 25 [API-SURFACE] + Phase 26 [MOBILE + PWA] + Phase 27 [REPORTING + BI] all closed 18/18 with zero deferrals. Next: Tier 4 product-depth cycles.
